items.title: Cheat Sheet: Charting Euphoria's long and messy road to season three
items.summary: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

items.title: Truck driver charged over Sunshine Coast crash that killed three-year-old girl
items.summary: A truck driver has been charged with drug driving over a crash that killed a three-year-old girl and critically injured her one-year-old sister.

items.title: Search for missing Murwillumbah woman Yvonne Whear continues in northern NSW
items.summary: The daughter of Yvonne Whear, 73, who went missing in February, says there is no evidence to suggest her mum left her northern NSW home of her own accord.

items.title: Russian President Vladimir Putin declares ceasefire in Ukraine for Orthodox Easter
items.summary: The Russian president declared the ceasefire following Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy proposing one earlier this week.

items.title: Live: Wall Street, oil prices up as investors consider Middle East peace plan
items.summary: A relief rally has continued on Wall Street overnight as investors mull the two-week ceasefire deal in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Mark Gregory's mum just wants to know what happened to her son 35 years ago
items.summary: Mark Gregory was just 28 when he was killed by a hit-and-run driver while walking home from a party in 1991. His family just wants answers.

items.title: Low-sensory voting to be introduced statewide at 2026 Victorian election
items.summary: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.

items.title: Queensland independent MP Jimmy Sullivan found dead
items.summary: MP for Stafford Jimmy Sullivan has been found dead in Brisbane.

items.title: Southern Queensland farmers take pre-emptive action as dry spell stretches on
items.summary: Queensland farmers gripped by a dry spell are turning to drastic and inventive measures: offloading most of their livestock and laying kilometres of water pipelines.

items.title: Study finds Tasmanian native forest logging increases potential for more severe bushfires
items.summary: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

items.title: Live: Australian role in Hormuz depends on permanent ceasefire: Marles
items.summary: Australia has been involved in talks about assisting the re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz but any action can only take place if a ceasefire becomes permanent, Richard Marles says. Follow live.

items.title: How the Parkes Dish is helping track Artemis II on its historic journey
items.summary: The Parkes Observatory — also known as The Dish — continues a legacy of supporting lunar missions that started more than 50 years ago, helping Artemis II go where humanity has not since 1972.

items.title: Masters 2026: Jason Day among early leaders, 2025 winner Rory McIlroy returns to top of leaderboard
items.summary: Jason Day kicks off his Masters tilt with a 3-under opening round, while 2013 champion Adam Scott is among a host of players to card eagles on day one at Augusta.

items.title: Tiwi Island birder hopes rare photos of endemic owl encourage conservation
items.summary: A traditional owner on a Northern Australian island has captured rare photos of an "enchanting" masked owl, adding to the limited knowledge base surrounding the endemic and culturally significant bird.

items.title: NSW drug diversion scheme disproportionately sends Aboriginal people to court, report says
items.summary: A BOSCAR report finds NSW Police disproportionately used "discretion" to reject eligible low-level drug offenders from a diversion program — especially Aboriginal people.

items.title: Melania Trump criticises Jeffrey Epstein 'lies' and calls for public hearings
items.summary: In an unusual White House address, Melania Trump denies having a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and complains about "mean-spirited" attempts to smear her.

items.title: Melania Trump denies any relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Safety review links herb in popular cold and flu supplements with life-threatening anaphylaxis
items.summary: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

items.title: Israel's backflip on Lebanon may be posturing, but it's keeping the fragile peace. What you need to know on Iran today
items.summary: The need to ensure the Iran ceasefire holds until the first round of peace talks are held has put Israel under pressure.

items.title: Three Tasmanian MPs share their experiences with mental health and politics
items.summary: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

items.title: Adelaide GP Mark Rogers disqualified for professional misconduct amid 'blatant disregard' for previous orders
items.summary: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

items.title: Sexual misconduct findings permanently added to health practitioners' records
items.summary: More than 100 past and current practitioners have had findings of sexual misconduct added to their public-facing records under new reforms to promote transparency.

items.title: Broken Hill sprinter Olufemi Komolafe's journey to 2026 Stawell Gift win
items.summary: Olufemi Komolafe is Broken Hill's first winner of the Stawell Gift since 1949. The 21-year-old sprinter and med student took out the men’s final at the iconic 2026 foot race in Western Victoria over the Easter long weekend.

items.title: Cyclone Maila weakens to category three as it slowly heads to Queensland
items.summary: Cyclone Maila has stalled again, pushing back the expected time the category three system will make landfall to Tuesday.

items.title: Teenage boy charged over Ipswich crash that killed passenger
items.summary: A teenage driver has been charged with dangerous driving after a crash that killed his 16-year-old passenger.

items.title: Jog for Joe aims to raise awareness of male eating disorders in Australia
items.summary: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men are at risk.

items.title: Police arrest three people over fatal shooting of man at Box Hill tower
items.summary: Two men from Bonnie Brook and a woman from Maryborough have been arrested over the death of a 32-year-old man in a suspected targeted shooting.

items.title: Goalkeeper Andrew Redmayne marks 300th A-League game on home turf
items.summary: His career began as a substitute for Mark Bosnich almost 20 years ago, now Andrew Redmayne is joining the A-League's exclusive 300 club.

items.title: Racism erupts after Melbourne announces Little India plans, but some Indians say it will build connection

items.title: Families demand refunds as Werribee Easter fun day described as a 'rip-off'
items.summary: A Melbourne council is investigating an Easter event held at the Werribee racecourse after it was criticised by attendees, who said it felt unsafe, underwhelming and poorly organised.

items.title: Taylor and Canavan are chalk and cheese – and that's a problem for Taylor
items.summary: If you want a basic measure of the difference between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, compare their reactions to US President Donald Trump's extraordinary threat this week that "a whole civilisation will die tonight".

items.title: Australian travellers fingerprinted and face-scanned as Europe's new border rules go live
items.summary: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.

items.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk of eroding – it's close to collapse
items.summary: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

items.title: TelstraSuper closes Direct Access investment product without properly notifying members
items.summary: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

items.title: Construction sector warns of housing delays, mass lay-offs due to Middle East war
items.summary: Contractors, business owners and economists warn that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

items.title: Illegal and legal wildlife trade fuelling animal-to-human disease transmission, report finds
items.summary: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

items.title: How the PM's Singapore visit could help Australia's fuel supply
items.summary: Experts say visits like Anthony Albanese's trip to Singapore are important for securing Australia's energy supplies. As one observer puts it: "When you don't have the stock, you need friends."

items.title: Inside the church of Pete Hegseth, the man who has been leading the US through war
items.summary: The ABC visits US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's church, whose leaders challenge the legitimacy of democracy and key principles in the US constitution.

items.title: David Hallberg is leading the Australian Ballet into the future with passion and drive

items.summary: US President Donald Trump issues a fresh warning to Iran over charging ships using the Strait of Hormuz, as parts of Kuwait and Lebanon come under attack. Follow live.

items.title: NATO chief Mark Rutte defends alliance as Trump threatens US withdrawal over Iran

items.summary: King Charles has written an open letter to Northern Territory residents following recent devastating flooding, saying the "increasingly catastrophic effects of climate change are surely a siren-call to the world for more urgent action".

items.title: High Court rejects BHP's attempt to appeal Same Job Same Pay order

items.summary: Grace Jarvis talks about everything from her experiences with autism, ADHD, queerness, and working in a sex shop in her stand-up. Her experiences with chronic illness? Not so much.

items.summary: Daniil Medvedev endures an eventful day at the Monte Carlo Masters, smashing several racquets before suffering the first 6-0, 6-0 trouncing of his distinguished career.

items.summary: Respectfully biting his tongue, Nick Faldo is refusing to dignify fresh criticism from Greg Norman that is certain to reignite one of golf's fiercest rivalries.

items.title: St John Ambulance NT reaches 'critical system failure' for second time in a week, calls for more resources
items.summary: The Northern Territory's ambulances service has reached "critical system failure" for the second time in a week, as a St John NT director says the service is so under-resourced its operations are at risk of failure "all the time".

items.title: Pet dog survives crocodile attack at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley
items.summary: A pet dog has a lucky escape after being dragged underwater by a freshwater crocodile at Lake Argyle in WA's remote Kimberley.

items.title: Jackie O complained about Kyle Sandilands months before on-air fight, court documents allege
items.summary: Court documents allege Jackie "O" Henderson was subject to "offensive and degrading" comments about her personal and dating life from co-host Kyle Sandilands in two separate incidents last year.

items.title: Bones discovered during bus stop construction work on Rottnest Island, sparking investigation
items.summary: Police are investigating the origin of several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island.

items.title: Coroner hears of police failings following 1976 disappearance of Veronica Green
items.summary: Ms Green was last seen alive on February 13, 1976, with the Coroner's Court of Victoria hearing the litany of policing errors following her disappearance.

items.title: Donald Trump cedes ground to Iran before negotiations even begin
items.summary: By defining the framework set out by Iran as "workable", the US president has ceded ground before negotiations have even begun.

items.title: Drunk driver jailed over hit-and-run death which led to changes in WA road laws
items.summary: An intoxicated driver who killed a woman in a hit-and-run in Mandurah, and was allowed to drive two months later, is sentenced to more than four years in prison.

items.title: The far side revealed: Artemis II's lunar fly-by in pictures
items.summary: A record-breaking voyage, an earthset in silence, and a total solar eclipse — astronauts on the Artemis II mission experienced sights no human has witnessed in half a century.

items.title: When is the best time to get your flu shot? Two infectious diseases experts explain
items.summary: When working out the best time to get your vaccine, you may be balancing a theoretical benefit by waiting against the chance of actually getting the flu before you get vaccinated.

items.title: Police investigate alleged on-field punch that left Victorian footballer with broken jaw

items.summary: Already, the 2026 premiership looks like it's Penrith's to lose, and there are three big reasons behind the Panthers' record-breaking start to the season.

items.title: Driver seriously injured, Princes Freeway shut for hours after truck collision at Officer
items.summary: The crash shutdown outbound lanes of the Princes Freeway at Officer in Melbourne's south-east.

items.title: SA farmers gamble on early winter crops after record rain, but expensive fuel and fertiliser a big concern
items.summary: Some SA farmers have rolled the dice on planting winter crops weeks earlier than usual thanks to record-breaking rains. Now they hope the gamble pays off.

items.summary: The South Australian premier is pushing for the state to be awarded a long-term contract for Gather Round, but says it could take weeks before a deal about the future of the footy festival is struck.

items.summary: An emergency Fair Work Commission hearing in Sydney begins, with unions and employers in the transport industry saying drivers, employees and businesses are on the verge of going under, calling on the top of the supply chain to share the load.

items.summary: Ex-Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley, who was fined for sledging in 2024, says he felt exploited by the league's later use of footage of his theatrics as a promotional tool.

items.title: Trump's escalating rhetoric inappropriate for a United States president, Albanese says

items.summary: Experts say half of the ACT's 6,000 beehives have been impacted by the fast-spreading parasite varroa mite with more than 1,000 destroyed.

items.title: Perth parents found guilty of starving teenage daughter granted bail ahead of possible retrial

items.title: Donald Trump sends JD Vance to save far-right Hungarian PM Viktor Orbán
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has sent his vice-president to Hungary in a bid to salvage the political future of far-right leader Viktor Orbán, who is flailing in the polls days before a closely watched election.

items.summary: Alex de Minaur is through to the final 16 of the Monte-Carlo Masters after defeating Britain's Cameron Norrie.

items.summary: The ex-CEO of Netball Australia said it was behind the times when she walked out the door in December 2023, but new gambling reforms announced by the federal government show the sport was right to steer clear from sports betting.

items.title: Talia Gibson jumps at chance to spearhead Australia against Britain in Billie Jean King Cup
